title: permissions.defaultMode: acceptEdits in settings.json -> same behavior as the CLI flag (route equivalence, incl. the cwd boundary)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/i-defaultMode-in-settings/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p1i-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call is waiting for permission to write to `$HOME/p1i-proof.txt`. Please approve it to proceed.

hypothesis: The mode can be set via settings.json (permissions.defaultMode) as well as via the CLI flag. With defaultMode: acceptEdits and NO --permission-mode flag, behavior must equal P1-b: write-cwd auto-approved, write-home still ask (the cwd boundary comes with the mode, not with the route). This closes the gap that P1's banner says 'settings 差分でモードを変える' while b-f actually vary a CLI flag.
